title: polypodium laxifolium
intro: Polypodium laxifolium, also known as the Indian fern or rock fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a perennial fern with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Polypodium laxifolium can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in decoctions, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
